title: 7 Herbal Tinctures For Bleeding Gums
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures can be a great way to soothe bleeding gums. When you drink herbal tea, the active ingredients in the herbs get into your bloodstream and travel to your gums, helping to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. This can be especially helpful if you have bleeding gums due to gum disease, which is caused by bacteria building up on your teeth.  One herb that can help with bleeding gums is Echinacea purpurea. It's known for its anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in your gums. Echinacea also has antiviral properties, which can help to fight off infections that can cause bleeding gums.  Another herb that can be helpful is Achillea millefolium, also known as yarrow. It has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection in your gums. Yarrow also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in your gums.  Salvia officinalis, or sage, can also be helpful for bleeding gums. It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to prevent infection and reduce swelling in your gums. Additionally, sage has been shown to help improve blood flow to your gums, which can help to promote healing.  When you use herbal tinctures to soothe bleeding gums, you may notice a few benefits. For one, your gums may become less inflamed and painful, making it easier to eat and speak. You may also notice that your breath becomes fresher, which can be a big confidence booster. Additionally, using herbal tinctures can help to prevent bleeding gums from becoming a bigger problem, such as gum disease, which can lead to tooth loss.  By using herbal tinctures to soothe bleeding gums, you can take a proactive approach to your oral health. This can help you to feel more confident and comfortable in your own skin, and can even help to prevent more serious problems down the line.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures contains alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds, such as alkylamides and polysaccharides, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums. The flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that combat gum inflammation and promote healing. The phenolic acids, like caffeic and ferulic acids, also have antimicrobial properties that prevent gum infections. The combination of these active constituents in Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps to soothe and heal bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Echinacea purpurea roots, 2 cups of 80% vodka, and a glass jar.
Combine the dried Echinacea purpurea roots and vodka in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Echinacea purpurea tincture in a cool, dark place. Use 20 drops, 3 times a day, for bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures contains bioactive constituents like sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce bleeding gums. The sesquiterpene lactones, specifically achilleine and alantolactone, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections. The flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have antioxidant properties that help to protect the gums from damage and promote healing. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Achillea millefolium tinctures can help to promote healthy gums and reduce bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Achillea millefolium flowers, 2 cups of 80-proof vodka or glycerin, and a clean glass jar with a lid.
Combine the flowers and vodka or glycerin in the jar, making sure the flowers are completely covered.
Stir the mixture and seal the jar. Store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Label the jar with the date, contents, and dosage instructions (typically 10-20 drops, 2-3 times a day). Store it in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid and carnosic acid, which are powerful antioxidants. These antioxidants help reduce inflammation in the gums, preventing bleeding and swelling. The antimicrobial properties of Salvia officinalis tinctures, particularly its content of ursolic acid, also help fight off bacteria and infections that can cause gum bleeding. Additionally, the tannins and flavonoids in the tincture help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Salvia officinalis tinctures can help promote healthy gums and prevent bleeding.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 2 cups of 80-proof vodka.
Combine the leaves and vodka in a glass jar. Stir well and seal the jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container. Discard the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-10 drops of the tincture under the tongue, 3 times a day, for bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures contains bioactive constituents like triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids that help reduce inflammation and fight infection in bleeding gums. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of these compounds help soothe and calm irritated gums, promoting healing and preventing further infection. The flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins in Calendula officinalis tinctures also have astringent properties, which help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of carotenoids in Calendula officinalis tinctures help protect gum tissue from oxidative damage, promoting healthy gum growth. By reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting healthy gum growth, Calendula officinalis tinctures can provide relief from bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers and 2 cups of 80% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Calendula flowers to the jar and cover them with vodka. Make sure they're completely covered.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place and use 10-20 drops, 2-3 times a day, for bleeding gums. Dilute with water if needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis tinctures contains rosmarinic acid, apigenin, and luteolin, which are key bioactive constituents that help with bleeding gums. Rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the gums, while apigenin and luteolin have antimicrobial properties that fight off bacteria that can cause infections. The tannins in Melissa officinalis also help to reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and promoting clotting. Additionally, Melissa officinalis has antiseptic properties that help to prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. By reducing inflammation, fighting off bacteria, and promoting clotting, Melissa officinalis tinctures can help to alleviate bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers, and 2 cups of 95% vodka in a clean glass jar.
Add the Melissa officinalis leaves and flowers to the jar and fill it with vodka, making sure the plants are completely covered.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the plants infuse into the vodka.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Use 20-30 drops of the Melissa officinalis tincture in a glass of water 2-3 times a day to help soothe bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, and phenolic acids like ferulic acid and caffeic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tannins in Eucalyptus globulus tinctures also have astringent properties, which help stop bleeding by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the affected area. The essential oils in Eucalyptus globulus tinctures, such as eucalyptol, also have antimicrobial properties that help prevent infection and promote healing in the gums. This combination of properties makes Eucalyptus globulus tinctures a useful remedy for bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of 80% vodka in a clean glass container.
Add the Eucalyptus leaves to the container and make sure they are fully covered by vodka.
Store the container in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it every day to help the tincture mix.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the leaves.
Store the tincture in the fridge and use 5-10 drops in water 2-3 times a day for bleeding gums.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures contains glycyrrhizin, a key bioactive constituent that contributes to its effectiveness in addressing bleeding gums. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the gums, allowing them to heal faster. The tannins present in the tincture also have astringent properties, which help contract and tighten the gum tissue, promoting blood clotting and reducing bleeding. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has antimicrobial properties, which help combat the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and bleeding. By addressing inflammation, promoting clotting, and preventing infections, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can help alleviate bleeding gums.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal tinctures for bleeding gums
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots, 2 cups of vodka (at least 60% alcohol), and a clean glass jar.
Crush the roots into small pieces using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder.
Combine the crushed roots and vodka in the glass jar. Stir well to mix.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. This is called 'steeping'.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle. Discard the solids. Store the tincture in a cool, dark place.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent bleeding gums. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have antiseptic properties that can reduce bacteria in the mouth, which can cause gum inflammation. Drinking these teas regularly may help keep your gums healthy and prevent bleeding. However, it's also important to maintain good oral hygiene habits.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can help soothe bleeding gums, consuming them daily may not be safe for everyone. Some herbal teas contain ingredients that can interact with medications or worsen certain health conditions. It's best to use them in moderation and monitor how your body reacts. Start with small amounts and adjust as needed.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with bleeding gums, but it takes time. Results can be seen within a few weeks to a month of regular consumption. Some people notice improvement in gum health within 2-3 weeks, while others may take longer. Consistency is key to seeing any benefits.
faq_time: When you have bleeding gums, it's a good idea to drink herbal tea in the morning. This helps reduce inflammation and kills bacteria that can cause pain and bleeding. Drinking it in the morning gives your body a chance to heal and recover throughout the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with certain prescription medications, including blood thinners and anti-inflammatory drugs, which are often used to treat bleeding gums. This is because some herbs, like ginkgo biloba and garlic, have blood-thinning properties that can increase the risk of bleeding when combined with other medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Bleeding gums can be a sign of a bigger issue, and some herbal teas might interact with it. For example, ginkgo biloba and foxglove can be harmful. If you still want to try, look for teas with gentle herbs like peppermint or chamomile, but drink them in moderation.
faq_breastfeeding: When you have bleeding gums while breastfeeding, it's understandable to want to try herbal teas for relief. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, are generally considered safe for breastfeeding mothers. However, it's essential to check the ingredients and ensure they don't contain any substances that can affect milk supply or harm your baby.
title_2: 7 Herbal Tinctures for Pregnant Women with Bleeding Gums
